message:
test(cli): Add comprehensive tests for config and offline modules

This commit introduces two new test files that significantly expand the
CLI test coverage for critical functionality: configuration management
and offline mode support.

=== config.test.ts (362 lines) ===

Tests the CLI configuration module (@/lib/config) which handles user
preferences, registry settings, and environment variable overrides.

Test categories:

1. Path Resolution (getHomeDir, getConfigDir)
   - Verifies JFP_HOME environment variable is respected
   - Ensures fallback to system homedir when not set
   - Validates config directory structure (~/.config/jfp/)

2. Default Configuration (createDefaultConfig)
   - Checks all required sections exist (registry, updates, skills,
     output, localPrompts, analytics)
   - Validates default registry URL (jeffreysprompts.com/api/prompts)
   - Ensures dynamic paths respect JFP_HOME
   - Verifies sensible defaults (autoRefresh: true, cacheTtl: 3600,
     color: true, analytics: false)

3. Config Loading (loadConfig)
   - Returns defaults when no config file exists
   - Loads custom config from file
   - Handles corrupt JSON gracefully (falls back to defaults)
   - Deep merges partial configs with defaults

4. Config Saving (saveConfig)
   - Creates config directory if missing
   - Saves valid JSON that can be loaded back
   - Merges with existing config on subsequent saves

5. Environment Variable Overrides
   - JFP_REGISTRY_URL overrides registry.url and registry.remote
   - JFP_CACHE_TTL overrides registry.cacheTtl (with NaN fallback)
   - JFP_NO_COLOR disables color output
   - Env vars take precedence over file config

6. Config Roundtrip
   - Full config survives save/load cycle with all fields intact

Test infrastructure:
- Uses unique temp directories per test (avoids test interference)
- Properly saves/restores environment variables in beforeEach/afterEach
- Cleans up test directories after each test

=== offline.test.ts (426 lines) ===

Tests the CLI offline mode module (@/lib/offline) which enables the CLI
to function without network connectivity using a local prompt cache.

Test categories:

1. Path Resolution
   - getLibraryDir returns correct path based on JFP_HOME
   - getLibraryPath points to prompts.json
   - getMetaPath points to sync.meta.json

2. Library Detection (hasOfflineLibrary)
   - Returns false when library doesn't exist
   - Returns true when library exists
   - Works correctly with empty prompt arrays

3. Sync Metadata (readSyncMeta)
   - Returns null when meta file doesn't exist
   - Returns parsed metadata when file exists
   - Handles corrupt JSON gracefully

4. Library Reading (readOfflineLibrary)
   - Returns empty array when library doesn't exist
   - Returns all prompts when library exists
   - Handles corrupt JSON gracefully
   - Preserves all prompt fields (id, title, content, description,
     category, tags, saved_at)

5. Prompt Lookup (getOfflinePromptById)
   - Returns null when library doesn't exist
   - Returns null for non-existent prompt IDs
   - Returns correct prompt by ID

6. Search Functionality (searchOfflineLibrary)
   - Finds prompts by title, ID, description, category, tags, content
   - Case insensitive search
   - Respects limit parameter
   - Sorts by relevance (title matches rank higher)
   - Returns empty array for no matches

7. Sync Age Formatting (formatSyncAge)
   - Returns "never" for null/undefined
   - Returns "unknown" for invalid dates
   - Returns "just now", "X min ago", "X hours ago", "X days ago"

8. Category Normalization (normalizePromptCategory)
   - Returns valid categories unchanged
   - Normalizes case
   - Falls back to "workflow" for invalid categories

9. Cache Integrity
   - Handles large libraries (100+ prompts)
   - Handles prompts with missing optional fields
   - Handles special characters in content ({{variables}}, HTML, etc.)

Sample test data includes realistic prompts:
- idea-wizard: Brainstorming assistant
- code-reviewer: Code review expert
- doc-writer: Documentation generator

Both test files follow the project's testing conventions:
- bun:test for test framework
- Isolated temp directories per test
- Environment variable save/restore pattern
- Comprehensive edge case coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
